# Codex Parity Matrix

| Area | Feature | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Core | Threads and transcript persistence | Implemented | JSON thread store, desktop bootstrap loading, and mock agent runner. |
| Core | Project picker/sidebar | Partial | Workspace state model, persisted project registry, selected-project context, native project rail, Codex-like New chat/Search primary nav, quieter Workspace footer for Plugins/Automations/Terminal/Browser/Memories/Activity/Command palette, desktop folder picker, current-directory desktop project seed, typed Local/SSH Remote project connections, project row actions for New chat, Refresh context, Rename, and Remove from list, SSH Remote Refresh context over the existing SSH executor, thread selection, visible rename/duplicate/pin/archive/unarchive/delete controls, bulk select/select-all/pin/unpin/archive/unarchive/delete actions with inactive Select collapsed into the Chats header, pinned/recent/archived chat grouping before the lower Projects section, archived-state search, command-palette/slash routing, and Playwright coverage exist. Richer sidebar drag/drop, saved filters, and full Codex workspace list polish pending. |
| Core | TrustedRouter runtime | Partial | CLI and desktop can use live TrustedRouter when an env or stored secret key exists; CLI auth key management and native developer settings exist; desktop has an explicit OAuth-first auth mode, loopback PKCE sign-in action, pure authorize/exchange/userinfo OAuth client, persisted delegated key storage, non-secret signed-in account display, and developer override as a secondary mode; desktop refreshes the live model catalog when keyed. Agent runs now expose incremental thread progress, consume live TrustedRouter action text as a stream, normalize common near-miss tool-call aliases back to canonical schema keys, show Streaming status while chunks arrive, render safe `say` action text as a live assistant draft without leaking tool JSON, support bounded multi-step tool continuation with hidden tool-feedback messages, surface actionable runtime issue cards/pills for missing sign-in, missing developer key, rejected key, rate limit, network failure, empty response, malformed model action, and generic run failure, can retry the latest user turn through the same send path, opens the searchable model picker directly from malformed-response and rate-limit recovery, and shows redacted runtime diagnostics plus parsed rate-limit metadata in Settings. Account recovery telemetry and provider status diagnostics beyond local runtime state pending. |
| Core | TrustedRouter model picker | Partial | Catalog model, live fetch adapter, searchable grouped provider/category/model SwiftUI picker, keyboard result highlighting with ArrowUp/ArrowDown plus Enter selection, persistent user favorites, recent-model surfacing from thread history, current/default/recommended/favorite badges, deterministic provider/category/model/default/recommended/favorite metadata rows, inline provider-detail browsing, duplicate-free normal search over row metadata, and Playwright coverage exist. Richer live provider capability metadata pending. |
| Tools | Shell commands | Implemented | `host.shell.run` with empty-command guard, workspace-bounded `cwd`, bounded `timeoutSeconds`, and bounded structured `environment` overrides. Local projects execute in the selected workspace; SSH Remote projects execute agent shell calls through noninteractive SSH from the remote root and reject unavailable local-only tool calls instead of silently touching the desktop workspace. |
| Tools | File read/write | Implemented | Workspace-scoped UTF-8 files. |
| Tools | Apply patch | Implemented | Workspace-scoped unified diff application through `git apply`. |
| Tools | Git status/diff/stage/restore/commit/push/PR/worktrees | Partial | Status/diff, file-level stage/restore, hunk-level stage/restore, commit, conservative named-remote branch push, GitHub PR creation via structured `gh pr create`, GitHub PR view/comments via `gh pr view --comments`, GitHub PR checks via `gh pr checks`, PR diff via `gh pr diff`, PR checkout via `gh pr checkout`, top-level GitHub PR comments via `gh pr comment`, GitHub PR review submissions via `gh pr review`, inline GitHub PR review comments via `gh api` with resolved PR/repo metadata, reviewer requests/removals via `gh pr edit --add-reviewer/--remove-reviewer`, label add/removal via `gh pr edit --add-label/--remove-label`, PR merge/automerge via `gh pr merge`, and git worktree list/create/open/remove/prune are implemented locally and for SSH Remote projects with workspace/path/patch/ref-name/PR-selector/checkout-branch/comment-body/review-action/review-comment-line/reviewer/label/merge-method checks. SSH Remote apply-patch/review actions refresh remote diff after successful changes. Broader GitHub review-session UX and inline comment reply/resolution workflows pending. |
| Safety | Read-only/Review/Auto modes | Implemented | Auto can use static or model reviewer; SwiftUI shell can switch and persist modes. |
| Safety | Reviewer model call | Partial | TrustedRouter client, native OAuth/developer settings state, and desktop loopback OAuth wiring exist. Rich reviewer telemetry and production prompt tuning pending. |
| UX | Tool cards | Partial | Tool-card presentation model, HTML harness, and SwiftUI shell render queued/running/done/failed/review states with a shared density contract: queued/running cards peek, completed cards collapse, and failed/review cards expand by default; project-bound tool cards carry shared execution-context metadata and render local/SSH Remote chips plus a thin context rail so old transcript scrollback still says where work ran; agent progress callbacks update the selected thread while a run is in flight; completed tool calls also produce human final-answer bubbles for common actions while full raw output stays behind card details; transcript rendering now interleaves messages and tool cards chronologically from the event timeline and preserves scroll intent across appends; hidden model-facing tool feedback is filtered from transcript surfaces, sidebar search, fork seeds, and compaction summaries; tool-result artifacts are surfaced as visible file/URL preview chips, local source/text artifacts render bounded UTF-8 previews, image-like artifacts render bounded previews, PR links render from structured artifacts, and PDF/document/spreadsheet/presentation/appshot artifacts render structured preview cards in SwiftUI, static HTML, and the Playwright harness; live TrustedRouter runs surface Streaming status before queued tools/final answers; safe streamed `say` text updates the assistant bubble before finalization; stopped queued/running tools close as failed `Stopped by user` cards; messages and tool cards expose lightweight Copy/Copy output actions with visible `Copied` feedback, user messages can be reused as focused composer drafts, assistant messages expose Helpful/Not helpful feedback backed by thread events, and the latest assistant response exposes a Retry action that reuses the normal retry command in SwiftUI, static HTML, and the Playwright harness. The Playwright harness now enforces first-pass interface polish primitives for smoothing, wrapping, tabular counters, hit areas, transitions, press feedback, radii, card density, image outlines, execution-context chips/rails, bottom-anchored empty state, and mid-scroll/bottom-pinned transcript append behavior. Broader native QuillUI polish and full embedded document/media renderers pending. |
| UX | Activity/task pane | Partial | A right-side Activity pane is derived from the selected thread event log, tool cards, active instructions, memories, artifacts, latest assistant answer, deterministic task plan, optional model-authored `host.plan.update` task plan, and handoff summary. SwiftUI, static HTML, and the Playwright harness share the same surface contract, including command-driven collapsible Task Plan/Recent/Handoff/Tools/Sources/Artifacts/Latest Answer sections, and Playwright covers task/source/tool/artifact/plan/handoff rendering plus collapse behavior. Richer task dependencies, nested plans, and model-authored handoff summaries remain pending. |
| UX | Review pane | Partial | Latest completed `host.git.diff` is summarized into a changed-file pane in SwiftUI and the Playwright harness; successful `host.apply_patch` runs immediately refresh `host.git.diff` so edits flow into review; file-level and hunk-level Stage/Restore controls execute workspace-scoped git tools locally or over SSH Remote projects and refresh the matching diff; file-scoped notes plus line and range review comments are replayed from thread events and hidden when stale. Structured inline GitHub PR review comments can now be posted from agent tools and `/pr review-comment`; richer GitHub review-session UI, reply, and resolution workflows pending. |
| UX | Top bar widget | Partial | Top-bar presentation state, HTML harness, SwiftUI shell, and macOS `MenuBarExtra` track thread/project/model/mode/status/Computer Use and expose quick New Chat, Open Project, Command Palette, Terminal, Browser, Settings, Stop All, and real Disconnect All affordances for active MCP servers and selected SSH Remote projects. The main workspace top bar is intentionally quiet: one-line thread identity leads, status is a dot/status popover, overflow is a single ellipsis with a bounded utility menu for Command Palette, Search, Computer Use setup, Settings, Keyboard Shortcuts, and Disconnect All only when it can act, while long instruction/memory/Computer Use/project labels remain bounded metadata instead of a dashboard row. Model selection and Auto/Review/Read-only now live beside the composer as send-time controls in SwiftUI, static HTML, and the Playwright harness, with model/mode kept independent. Stop All cancels active sends, integrated-terminal commands, and active MCP servers; Disconnect All shares that cleanup and also detaches selected SSH Remote context without removing the project. Rich live progress, agent-router process cancellation, QuillCloud remote controls, and Linux tray adapter pending. |
| UX | Context and rate banners | Partial | Estimated context pressure banner appears near the local budget and offers Compact context, New thread, and Fork from last. Compact context creates a bounded continuation thread while preserving project/mode/model/instructions/memories. Exact provider token accounting, model-backed summaries, and richer fork context controls pending. |
| UX | Keyboard shortcuts/slash commands | Partial | Local slash command parser handles `/help`, `/status`, `/new`, `/rename title`, `/duplicate`, `/archive`, `/unarchive`, `/project new|refresh|rename|remove`, `/ssh user@host:/path`, `/compact`, `/mode`, `/model`, `/terminal`, `/terminal clear`, `/browser`, `/worktrees`, `/worktree create|open|remove|prune`, `/pr create|view|checks|diff|checkout|comment|review|review-comment|reviewers|labels|merge`, `/memories`, `/remember text`, and `/env [name]`; workspace-action slash commands share the same model/tool paths as visible UI and the command palette, and PR/worktree slash subcommands dispatch through the same structured local/SSH Remote `host.git.*` tools as agent calls. The composer now shows filtered slash suggestions from the same catalog as `/help`, with keyboard selected-row navigation, Enter/Tab accept behavior, click-to-insert command skeletons, and multiline textarea behavior with Shift+Enter newlines plus Enter-to-send in SwiftUI, static HTML, and the Playwright harness. Search opens a finder over thread title, model, pinned/archived state, and indexed transcript/tool content with focused input, highlighted results, ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigation, and Enter selection in SwiftUI and the Playwright harness. Active chat Find is implemented as a `Cmd+F` transcript bar with result counts, next/previous navigation, focused input, active-result highlighting, and command-palette access. A grouped command palette over workspace commands exists in SwiftUI and the Playwright harness, with registry-backed shortcuts, keyword/shortcut/title ranking, selected-row keyboard navigation, `>` action scoping, `/` slash-command scoping with visible scope badges, and slash-template insertion that returns focus to the composer; E2E coverage verifies primary workspace actions and scoped slash insertion. `Cmd+/` opens a command-derived Keyboard Shortcuts panel in SwiftUI, desktop menus, menu bar, and the Playwright harness. Complete Codex shortcut parity pending. |
| Workspace | Integrated terminal | Partial | Workspace-scoped terminal pane, command history with Up/Down recall that preserves partially typed drafts, live stdout/stderr streaming for running commands, persisted execution context per terminal entry, persistent per-project working directory and exported/unset environment deltas for local projects, SSH Remote terminal execution through noninteractive `ssh -T -o BatchMode=yes`, remote cwd/env continuity across terminal commands through stripped marker metadata, explicit running/done/failed/stopped command lifecycle, terminal-local Stop, Clear history, `/terminal clear`, Stop All integration, cancellable command execution, native SwiftUI surface, static HTML rendering, and Playwright coverage exist; full interactive PTY job control and curses-style TUI handling pending. |
| Workspace | Worktrees | Partial | Core `git worktree` list/create/open/remove/prune tools, shared command-palette actions, create/open/remove dialogs, open/remove dialogs with known-worktree choices from `git worktree list --porcelain`, local and SSH Remote picker loading/error/retry states without transcript tool-card noise, safe dry-run cleanup command, review/confirm stale-worktree prune dialog, `/worktree create|open|remove|prune` slash commands, and create/open-time handoff into a selected local or SSH Remote worktree project/thread exist. Rich branch lifecycle polish and PR handoff remain pending. |
| Workspace | Local environment actions | Partial | Project-local `.quillcode/actions/*.sh` and `.quillcode/local-env/*.sh` scripts are discovered with symlink/root bounds and exposed in the command palette plus `/env [name]` slash command as `Run ...` actions that dispatch through `host.shell.run`. Optional same-name JSON sidecars provide display `title`, `description`, `order`, bounded `environment`, safe project-relative `workingDirectory`, and bounded `timeoutSeconds` metadata without changing which script executes; command search includes environment key names, cwd paths, and timeout labels, not values. Local-env commands now pass cwd, environment, and timeout as structured tool arguments instead of embedding shell-quoted command prefixes. Tool-card/transcript payloads keep environment key names while redacting values, and reruns read current project metadata instead of recovering secrets from thread history. Direct shell `cwd` arguments are normalized and rejected unless they resolve inside the selected workspace, `timeoutSeconds` is bounded to 1-1800 seconds, and direct `environment` overrides use the same key/value policy. Scheduling and richer local-env orchestration remain pending. |
| Workspace | AGENTS/rules instructions | Partial | Project-local and nested `AGENTS.md`, `.quillcode/rules.md`, and `.quillcode/instructions.md` are bounded, persisted on project refs, copied into thread context in broad-to-specific order, injected into TrustedRouter as hidden system context, and surfaced as a top-bar status. Instruction records carry derived applicability scopes, the prompt tells the model to apply scoped rules only under matching paths, and Activity sources show the applicable scope plus visible duplicate-scope and nested-override diagnostics. Semantic contradiction detection and richer conflict review UI pending. |
| Browser | In-app browser | Partial | Workspace browser panel, address normalization for web/localhost/file/project-relative targets, bounded local HTML metadata snapshots, bounded HTTP(S) HTML snapshot fetching for reachable web/localhost pages, explicit static-vs-network/live-DOM inspection-depth labels, visible page outlines, text snippets, web/localhost source summaries, browser comments, Back/Forward/Reload snapshot-history controls, SwiftUI surface, HTML renderer, structured `host.browser.open` and `host.browser.inspect` agent tools, a live DOM capture adapter contract, macOS desktop WebKit live-DOM capture for rendered HTTP(S) pages, persistent WebKit browser-profile reuse for cookies/session state by default, explicit ephemeral capture mode for future privacy/test controls, a reusable macOS visible WebKit browser-session window wired from the browser pane, menu bar, and command palette for signing into sites with the same persistent profile, and Playwright coverage exist. Multi-tab session management, session-to-preview synchronization beyond shared cookies, and Linux/browser-process capture remain pending. |
| Computer Use | Backend protocol | Partial | Backend protocol, deterministic stub, macOS permission-aware status, screenshot capture, mouse, keyboard, and scroll adapter exist. The desktop window and menu bar render specific missing-permission labels, Settings includes an actionable Computer Use card with Screen Recording, Accessibility, setup, and refresh commands, and agent runs can dispatch `host.computer.screenshot`, click, type, scroll, move, and key tools through the active backend with concise final answers. Screenshots are stored as artifacts instead of raw base64 in transcript details, render as image previews on tool cards, and bounded multi-step turns can feed screenshot metadata back to the model for the next action. Linux backend, app approvals, richer permission education, and visual screenshot interpretation pending. |
| Plugins | Skills/plugins/MCP | Partial | Project-local `.quillcode/plugins/*.json`, `.quillcode/skills/*.json`, and `.quillcode/mcp/*.json` manifests are discovered with symlink/root and byte/count bounds, surfaced in the Extensions pane, command palette, static HTML renderer, macOS menu bar, and Playwright harness. Manifests can expose version/source metadata and explicit bounded install/update commands that run through normal shell tool-card auditing, then refresh project metadata. MCP stdio manifests preserve structured command/args and can be explicitly started/stopped from the Extensions pane or command palette. Start performs a bounded MCP `initialize` plus `tools/list` probe before showing Ready, optionally probes advertised `resources/list` and `prompts/list`, surfaces server/protocol/tool/resource/prompt metadata including bounded per-tool input schema summaries, and Stop All terminates active MCP servers. Ready MCP servers expose generic allowlisted `host.mcp.call`, `host.mcp.resource.read`, and `host.mcp.prompt.get` agent tools that return MCP content into the normal tool-card/final-answer flow, and the dynamic tool description includes each ready MCP tool's required/optional argument schema. Ready MCP resources and prompts are also directly actionable from the Extensions pane and command palette, dispatching through the same live MCP runtime, transcript tool cards, and final-answer rendering as agent-authored calls. Marketplace browsing/install, MCP streaming, and executable plugin wiring pending. |
| Long-running | Automations/subagents | Partial | Automations now have a first-class command, primary sidebar entry, SwiftUI/static HTML/Playwright pane, a core `QuillAutomation` model, JSON persistence under `~/.quillcode/automations.json`, desktop bootstrap loading, configured-row rendering, command-palette/thread-pane creation of persisted thread follow-ups, quick schedules for 10 minutes, 1 hour, tomorrow morning, and daily recurrence, slash-command natural scheduling for bounded relative delays, tomorrow clock phrases, and `every N minutes/hours/days/weeks` recurrence, a manual `Run now` path that creates/selects a follow-up thread from the original conversation, selected-project workspace schedule creation, quick workspace-check schedules, `/workspace-check ...` natural scheduling through the same parser as thread follow-ups, a manual/due workspace schedule runner that creates/selects a scheduled workspace-check thread with project instructions and memories, `lastRunAt` recording, recurring jobs advancing `nextRunAt` instead of completing after one run, a desktop tick that auto-runs active due thread follow-ups and workspace schedules from persisted `nextRunAt`, native macOS local notifications for created automation threads, pause/resume/delete actions, and planned fallback rows for thread follow-ups, workspace schedules, and monitors. Monitor runners, subagents, broader calendar language, Linux notification adapters, and richer notification controls remain pending after the stable thread/workspace runtime. |
| Memory | Memories/Chronicle | Partial | Local memories load from `~/.quillcode/memories` and project `.quillcode/memories`, with extension allow-listing, symlink/root escape rejection, count/file/total byte caps, truncation labels, thread snapshots, TrustedRouter system-context injection, top-bar status, Memories pane, static HTML, Playwright coverage, slash-command visibility, command-palette Add memory prefill, `/remember text` explicit global writes, `/remember-edit` explicit global updates plus local and SSH Remote project-memory updates from memory-card Edit, agent-callable `host.memory.remember` writes for user-requested durable preferences/facts, Forget actions for global/local project/SSH Remote project memories, transcript/tool audit records, immediate context refresh, remote memory edits and deletes bounded to already loaded `.quillcode/memories` files over SSH with post-mutation context refresh, and basic credential/token/password/private-key rejection for writes and edits. Richer redaction review, idle Chronicle jobs, conflict UI, project-memory review UX, and fully autonomous memory inference without explicit user intent remain pending. |
| Remote | Phone/SSH remote | Partial | `ProjectConnection` persists typed local/SSH references, `/ssh user@host:/path` and `Project: Add SSH Remote...` register remote projects, the sidebar shows an `SSH Remote` badge, terminal labels render the remote address, integrated terminal commands run over noninteractive SSH from the remote root, agent `host.shell.run`, bounded `host.file.read`/`host.file.write`, `host.apply_patch`, `host.git.status`/`host.git.diff`, file/hunk `host.git.stage`/`host.git.restore`, `host.git.commit`, conservative `host.git.push`, `host.git.pr.create`/`view`/`checks`/`diff`/`checkout`/`comment`/`review`/`reviewers`/`labels`/`merge`, and `host.git.worktree.*` calls route through the same SSH executor, and explicit Refresh context loads bounded remote `AGENTS.md`, `.quillcode/rules.md`, `.quillcode/instructions.md`, and project `.quillcode/memories` files into the selected thread context. The agent receives only remote-safe tools for SSH Remote projects so unavailable tools cannot accidentally run locally, and remote shell/file/patch/git tool cards plus terminal entries show per-item SSH execution-context chips/rails. Remote approvals and QuillCloud/relay project execution remain pending. |
| Artifacts | Tool-result artifact links and previews | Partial | Tool-result artifacts now render as visible file/URL preview chips with labels and source details in SwiftUI, static HTML, and the Playwright harness. Local source/text file artifacts render bounded UTF-8 previews with binary, byte, line, and extension guards. Image-like file, web, and inline data artifacts render bounded visual previews with structured type/extension/source metadata below the chips. PDF, document, spreadsheet, presentation, and appshot artifacts render structured type/detail/open cards with shared model derivation; `.appshot.json` bundles are classified as Appshots rather than noisy raw JSON previews. Full embedded renderers for PDFs, docs, sheets, slides, appshots, and richer generated-media dimensions/history remain pending. |
